ogTitle: Back Valley vs Baird Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Back Valley and Baird Bay.
heading: Back Valley vs Baird Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Back Valley, South Australia and Baird Bay, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Back Valley has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Baird Bay (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Back Valley or Baird Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Back Valley has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Baird Bay scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Back Valley scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Baird Bay scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Back Valley or Baird Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Back Valley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
